title: Macramé baby mobile
subtitle: A gentle nursery mobile in natural cotton cord with hanging shapes and fringing
excerpt: Knot a simple nursery mobile in natural 3 mm cotton cord, hanging from a small wooden dowel or branch. Six hanging elements (moons, stars, and cloud shapes formed in cord and cardboard) suspend at different heights on knotted hangers.
sourceNotes: Synthesised from contemporary macramé practice. Nursery mobiles in natural cotton cord are a current British macramé guild project; the hanging-shape construction follows standard macramé pattern conventions.

glossaryTerms.definition: The core macramé knot: two working cords tied around two filler cords, first left over right, then right over left. Used here for the hanging elements and the main suspension cords.
A mounting knot for attaching folded cords to a dowel or ring. The folded cord loop passes over the dowel and the two tails are pulled through the loop and down.
The unworked ends of cord that hang below the last knot in a macramé piece. Fringe can be left natural, trimmed to a point, or brushed out to a soft tassel.

content.text: A macramé baby mobile hangs from a wooden dowel. Six cords descend at different heights, each carrying a small shaped piece. Use a
to mount cords on the dowel.

items.name: Wooden dowel or smooth branch, 30 cm long
Thin cardboard (for shape templates: moon, star, cloud)
White craft glue or fabric glue

content.text: Step 1: Prepare the dowel and main suspension

Cut two 80 cm lengths of cord and tie them together at the centre to form a crossed suspension above the dowel. Tie the four tails to the four positions along the dowel using lark's head knots, spacing the attachment points evenly. The mobile will balance from the central crossing point.
Step 2: Make the cardboard shape frames

Cut two moon shapes (crescent), two star shapes, and two cloud shapes from thin cardboard. These are the inner frames around which the cord is wrapped to build each hanging element. Make each shape about 8 to 10 cm across.
Step 3: Wrap and knot the hanging shapes

For each shape, cut a 2 m length of cord and wrap it tightly around the cardboard frame, covering the edges completely. Glue the end securely. For the moon shapes, add a short knotted fringe at the lower curve: mount two 20 cm lengths of cord with lark's head knots, tie a single square knot on each pair, and leave 5 cm of fringe below.
Step 4: Hang the shapes from the dowel

Cut six hanging cords in varying lengths (20 cm, 25 cm, 30 cm, 35 cm, 40 cm, 45 cm). Attach one end of each to a shape using a simple knot through the top of the cardboard frame, and the other end to the dowel with a lark's head knot. Space the shapes along the dowel, alternating shapes and heights for visual balance.

Hold the mobile by its central suspension and adjust the position of the shapes along the dowel until it balances level. Slide the lark's head knots along the dowel to reposition. Once balanced, add a small drop of glue to each knot to hold the position.

items.problem: The mobile does not balance and tips to one side
items.cause: The heavier shapes are too far toward one end of the dowel
items.fix: Slide all shapes slightly toward the lighter end. Balance is easiest to achieve when the heaviest shapes are closest to the centre of the dowel.

items.problem: The cord wrapping on the cardboard shapes unravels
items.cause: The cord ends were not glued securely
items.fix: Apply a small amount of glue to the starting and ending cord ends and press firmly. Allow to dry before hanging.
